note: Hölbl banner: 'ALEXANDER THE GREAT'. Accession: end of 332 BCE (invasion of Egypt). Death: 10 June 323 at Babylon. No 'Ptolemy as Satrap' annotation on this banner.

Hölbl banner: 'PHILIP III ARRHIDAIOS // Ptolemy as Satrap'. Accession at Division of empire at Babylon 323 BCE (Arrhidaios acknowledged as king Philip, Ptolemy receives satrapy of Egypt). End: Autumn 317, Murder of Philip III Arrhidaios.

Hölbl banner: 'ALEXANDER IV // Ptolemy as Satrap'. Banner begins 317 (after murder of Philip III Arrhidaios); ends at 310/09, Murder of Alexander IV by Kassandros. end_year uses earlier boundary of compound 310/09 notation per extraction rule.
